skill.name: wordpress
skill.description: Guided WordPress workflow for themes, plugins, WooCommerce stores, performance work, security checks, testing, and deployment planning.
skill.summary: Plan and coordinate production WordPress development workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks, skill names, code fences, and ordinary words such as design. No executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or credential access were found in the reviewed skill file. The skill should publish with a warning because the REST API example uses an unrestricted permission callback.

medium_findings.title: Public REST API Permission Example
medium_findings.description: The WordPress REST endpoint example uses permission_callback set to __return_true. This can be valid for public read-only data, but it teaches an unrestricted access pattern that may expose data if copied into sensitive endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code sample explicitly registers a REST route with an unrestricted permission callback. The example is a public GET endpoint, so the risk depends on how users adapt it.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed locations contain markdown inline skill names, copy-paste prompt fences, file tree examples, or PHP examples, not executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source file is markdown and the flagged backticks delimit text examples. No command runner, shell script, Ruby code, or dynamic execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography patterns in ordinary prose and identifiers. Reviewed lines contain terms such as description, design, and includes rather than DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or related cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic algorithm usage on the cited lines. The matches are caused by common English substrings and file names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted WordPress REST Permission Callback
dangerous_patterns.description: The example route allows access through __return_true. Users should replace this with a capability check or nonce validation for non-public data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The unrestricted callback is visible in the sample. It is dangerous when applied to privileged or private WordPress data.

content.user_title: Build WordPress Sites With Guided Workflows
content.value_statement: WordPress projects often span themes, plugins, stores, security, testing, and deployment. This skill organizes that work into practical phases and prompts.
actual_capabilities: Breaks WordPress projects into setup, theme, plugin, WooCommerce, optimization, security, testing, and deployment phases.
Lists supporting specialist skills to invoke for each workflow phase.
Provides copy-paste prompts for coordinating WordPress development tasks.
Defines practical checklists for performance, security, quality, and release readiness.
Shows basic examples for custom post types, REST endpoints, and WooCommerce product types.
limitations: It is a planning workflow, not an automated WordPress installer.
It does not include complete production code for themes or plugins.
It depends on other named skills for detailed implementation work.
Security examples require review before use on private or privileged data.

use_cases.title: Plan a New WordPress Build
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to sequence setup, theme work, plugin needs, testing, and deployment before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate WooCommerce Delivery
use_cases.description: Map store setup, payment integration, subscription logic, checkout customization, and release checks into clear tasks.

use_cases.title: Audit an Existing Site
use_cases.description: Review performance, security, testing, and deployment gaps with the included quality gates and checklists.

prompt_templates.title: Start a WordPress Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WordPress workflow to plan a new site. Include setup, theme work, plugin needs, testing, and deployment steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WordPress workflow to outline a custom theme build. Include template hierarchy, assets, responsive design, and quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a WooCommerce Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WordPress workflow to plan a WooCommerce implementation with products, checkout, payments, subscriptions, testing, and release validation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Production Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WordPress workflow to create a production release plan with security hardening, performance targets, tests, migrations, rollback, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Plan a new WordPress membership site.
output: A phased delivery plan covering setup, theme structure, membership plugin design, payments, security controls, tests, and deployment.
A checklist for launch readiness, including performance targets and access control review.

output_examples.input: Improve an existing WooCommerce store before launch.
output: A prioritized workflow for checkout review, Stripe integration, subscription setup, caching, browser testing, and production monitoring.
A list of specialist prompts to run for payments, billing, performance, and deployment.
A review plan covering plugin architecture, hooks, admin screens, REST routes, database tables, tests, and security checks.
Action items for capability checks, sanitization, logging, and release validation.

best_practices: Use the workflow as a project plan before writing implementation prompts.
Review every generated WordPress code sample for capabilities, sanitization, escaping, and nonce checks.
Run performance, security, accessibility, browser, and mobile checks before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not copy REST endpoint examples into private data routes without permission checks.
Do not treat the workflow as a replacement for WordPress security review.
Do not deploy generated theme or plugin code without testing in staging.

faq.question: Does this skill install WordPress?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow for planning and coordinating WordPress development tasks.

faq.question: Can it create themes and plugins?
faq.answer: It can guide theme and plugin work through prompts and structure examples. It does not provide complete production code.

faq.question: Does it support WooCommerce?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes WooCommerce planning steps for products, checkout, payments, subscriptions, and store release checks.

faq.question: Is this safe for production projects?
faq.answer: Use it for planning, then review generated code before deployment. REST routes and payment flows need careful security review.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare project goals, hosting constraints, required plugins, store needs, security requirements, and launch timeline.
